identity.short_name: Delta reduction gives Z; compute \sqrt(Z-1) from |∇ψ| at a point

subdomain(s): partial differential equations (Green functions for Laplacian)
topic_path: delta-function reduction on a defect surface

problem_spec.summary: A delta-supported defect-surface term collapses an integral and yields Z equal to the induced-metric determinant on a graph surface; compute \sqrt(Z-1) at a specified point from a given ψ(x₀,x₁).
givens: A 3D Euclidean Laplacian Green function G0(x,y) and a defect surface Σ given by x₂ = ψ(x₀,x₁).
The delta-supported term F collapses the y-integral so that Z(x_i) = (1/δ(0)) ∬ F(x_i,y_i) dy₀ dy₁ equals det(g_ab(x_i)) after δ(0) cancellation.
ask: Compute S = \sqrt(Z - 1) at the evaluation point.

parameter(s).name: psi
parameter(s).purpose: defines the defect surface and its induced metric via gradients

parameter(s).name: amplitude_pair
parameter(s).purpose: supplies explicit gradient values at the evaluation point

invariant(s).name: delta_pullback_collapse
invariant(s).statement: The F-term reduces to det(g_ab(x)) \cdot δ^(2)(x_i-y_i) \cdot δ(ψ(x)-ψ(y)); integrating over y_i yields det(g_ab(x)) \cdot δ(0), which cancels in Z by definition.

invariant(s).name: induced_metric_determinant
invariant(s).statement: For a graph surface in Euclidean space, g_ab = δ_ab + (∂_aψ)(∂_bψ), hence det(g_ab)=1+|∇ψ|² in (x₀,x₁).

invariant(s).name: nonnegativity_for_sqrt
invariant(s).statement: Z=det(g_ab)\geq1, so Z-1\geq0 and \sqrt(Z-1) is well-defined as the nonnegative root.

essential_assumption(s): The δ(0) renormalization cancels exactly as specified in the definition of Z.
ψ is differentiable so ∇ψ exists at the evaluation point.
The principal (nonnegative) square root is used for \sqrt(Z-1).
uniqueness_certificate(s): After the delta-function reduction, Z is uniquely determined by det(g_ab) at the evaluation point, which is uniquely determined by ∇ψ at that point; hence S=\sqrt(Z-1) is unique.

technique(s).name: integrate_using_delta_functions
inputs: Z definition as an integral of F
technique(s).purpose: collapse the y-integral and cancel the δ(0) factor

technique(s).name: compute_induced_metric_determinant_for_graph
technique(s).purpose: compute det(g_ab)=1+|∇psi|^2 for a graph surface
